Title: Aminoisothiazolamides, a new class of potent inhibitors of lysyl-tRNA synthetase

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: AtKRS1: UniProt Q9ZPI1; ScKRS1: NA; HsKRS: UniProt D32053
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class II aminoacyl-tRNA synthetase; N-terminal anticodon-binding domain and C-terminal catalytic domain
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Lysyl-tRNA formation; ATP-binding site of cytosolic lysyl-tRNA synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Core catalytic step in translation; amino acid activation/tRNA charging
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates L-lysine with ATP and transfers lysine to tRNA(Lys); in the assay also catalyzes lysine-dependent noncanonical Ap4A formation in the presence of Zn2+
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for synthesis/utilization of the nutrient amino acid lysine in protein biosynthesis; herbicidal target rather than nutrient-biofortification determinant
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point / validated biochemical target for aminoisothiazolamides
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cytoplasmic isoenzyme; detailed tissue pattern NA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lysine is a proteinogenic amino acid; Ap4A is a signaling/stress-associated nucleotide
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Zn2+ enabled detection of noncanonical Ap4A formation; pre-emergence herbicide treatment in glasshouse assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ScKRS1 overexpression reduced yeast sensitivity to aminoisothiazolamides, identifying KRS1 as a key biochemical target. 2. Arabidopsis KRS1 bound compound 1a strongly in thermal shift assays (Kd 6.4 ± 3.0 μM; 1.6 ± 0.9 μM with L-lysine), supporting ATP-site binding. 3. AtKRS1 catalyzed lysine-dependent Ap4A formation, enabling a colorimetric inhibition assay. 4. Inhibition of AtKRS1 across analogs correlated with herbicidal activity. 5. Binding-site residues are highly conserved across plants and mammals, limiting species selectivity and contributing to toxicological risk.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast overexpression library screening; ScKRS1 overexpression validation; thermal stability shift assay; X-ray co-crystal structures; enzymatic Ap4A assay; inhibitor SAR with metabolically relevant herbicidal phenotyping; sequence alignment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inhibition of KRS1 causes strong herbicidal activity of aminoisothiazolamides, but poor plant/mammal selectivity limits development
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper focuses on herbicide mode of action, not nutrient improvement. Compound 1a occupies the adenosine portion of the ATP-binding site. Active-site conservation across plants, mammals, fungi and some pathogens was emphasized.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe mammalian acute toxicity risk; limited crop selectivity; likely developmental toxicity and neurological liabilities from mammalian KRS inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low value for nutrient engineering; mainly useful as a target-discovery case showing KRS1 is not a viable herbicide target unless strong plant-specific selectivity can be achieved

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes lysine activation and tRNA charging; assayed as an additional plant KRS isoform for inhibitor sensitivity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for amino acid utilization in protein biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; auxiliary validated isoform in target profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pre-emergence herbicide treatment in glasshouse assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtKRS2 was purified and used as a second Arabidopsis lysyl-tRNA synthetase isoform in biochemical inhibition assays. 2. Aminoisothiazolamide inhibition of AtKRS2 correlated with inhibition of AtKRS1 and HsKRS. 3. Data supported broad cross-species conservation of target sensitivity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein purification; enzymatic inhibition assay across compound series; comparative correlation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtKRS2 is inhibited by aminoisothiazolamides but was not the primary mechanistic focus relative to AtKRS1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly to support target family sensitivity and conservation, not as the main causal target linked to herbicidal phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Same selectivity concern as KRS1 likely due to conservation
